parameters.name: xyzchop
parameters.label: XYZ CHOP
parameters.description: A with 3 channels is required to specify the X, Y, and Z position of the quad to be generated. Each sample creates 1 quad. The created quads are centered at the points defined by each sample.

parameters.name: camera
parameters.label: Camera
parameters.description: The geometry will always face the specified here.

parameters.name: widthchop
parameters.label: Width CHOP
parameters.description: A 1 channel can be specified to set the width of the quad at that sample.

parameters.name: colorchop
parameters.label: Color CHOP
parameters.description: A 3 channel can be specified to set the R, G, and B color values of the quad at that sample.

parameters.name: alphachop
parameters.label: Alpha CHOP
parameters.description: A 1 channel can be specified to set the alpha of the quad at that sample.

parameters.name: perspectivewidth
parameters.label: Perspective Width
parameters.description: The width of the geometry created. This parameter only has an effect when the is less that 1.0.

parameters.name: constantwidth
parameters.label: Use Constant Width
parameters.description: This blends between using Perspective Width and Constant Width. When Use Constant Width = 0, width is determined only by Perspective Width, when Use Constant Width = 1, width is determined only by Con

parameters.name: constantwidthnear
parameters.label: Constant Width Near
parameters.description: The width used for quads located at a position Constant Width Falloff Start or closer.

parameters.name: constantwitdhfar
parameters.label: Constant Width Far
parameters.description: The width used for quads located at a position Constant Width Falloff End or farther.

parameters.name: falloffstart
parameters.label: Constant Width Falloff Start
parameters.description: At this position the or closer the geometry uses the Constant Width Near parameter for its width.

parameters.name: falloffend
parameters.label: Constant Width Falloff End
parameters.description: At this position the or farther the geometry uses the Constant Width Far parameter for its width.
